hypotheses.name: Outage Caused SLI Breach
hypotheses.statement: The SLI breach on '{slo_sli_id}' for subscription '{subscription_id}' in region '{region}' (customer '{customer_name}') with {impacted_resources} impacted resources and avg value {avg_value_across_windows} was caused by an Azure outage affecting that region or service.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-OUT-003 (time overlap ≤60 min between incident ImpactStartDate and SLI EarliestImpactStart) co-occurs with SYM-OUT-002 (confirmed outage). Cross-region (SYM-SLI-005, ≥2 regions) or cross-subscription (SYM-SLI-004, ≥2 subscriptions) patterns strengthen the outage hypothesis. SYM-OUT-004 (incident linked to SupportTicketId) adds direct customer correlation.

hypotheses.name: Configuration Change Caused SLI Breach
hypotheses.statement: The SLI breach on '{slo_sli_id}' for subscription '{subscription_id}' in region '{region}' (customer '{customer_name}') with {impacted_resources} impacted resources over {total_impact_duration_min} min was caused by a recent configuration change or deployment.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-SLI-003 (≥2 distinct SLIs breached in same subscription+region) appears without cross-subscription or cross-region patterns, suggesting a localized change affected multiple service dimensions. Absence of SYM-OUT-001/002 (no matching incident) further supports this hypothesis.

hypotheses.name: Capacity Exhaustion Caused SLI Breach
hypotheses.statement: The SLI breach on '{slo_sli_id}' for subscription '{subscription_id}' in region '{region}' (customer '{customer_name}') with avg value {avg_value_across_windows} and min value {min_value_across_windows} was caused by capacity exhaustion — resource utilization exceeded safe thresholds.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-SLI-002 fires with CRITICAL severity (min_value=0, avg<1.0) or HIGH severity (min_value=0 or avg<10.0), indicating total or near-total resource exhaustion. High impacted_resources count with sustained total_impact_duration_min reinforces this.

hypotheses.name: Dependency Failure Caused SLI Breach
hypotheses.statement: The SLI breach on '{slo_sli_id}' for subscription '{subscription_id}' in region '{region}' (customer '{customer_name}') was caused by a failure in a downstream dependency — the breach pattern spans multiple subscriptions, regions, or SLIs sharing a common dependency.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-SLI-004 (≥2 subscriptions) or SYM-SLI-005 (≥2 regions) co-occur with SYM-SLI-003 (≥2 SLIs), indicating a shared upstream dependency failure propagating across boundaries. Absence of SYM-OUT-001/002 distinguishes this from a known outage.

hypotheses.name: Workload Spike Caused SLI Breach
hypotheses.statement: The SLI breach on '{slo_sli_id}' for subscription '{subscription_id}' in region '{region}' (customer '{customer_name}') with {impacted_resources} impacted resources over {total_impact_duration_min} min was caused by an unexpected workload spike exceeding capacity.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-SLI-002 (severe degradation) appears for a single subscription+region without cross-subscription (SYM-SLI-004) or cross-region (SYM-SLI-005) patterns. SYM-SUP-001 (support case filed) adds customer context. ER-LOAD-002 baseline comparison is critical to confirm abnormal traffic.
